card_description: Nick is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Nick and the Glimmung*. The titular Nick. Name note: Common name suggesting everyman. The ordinary name in the extraordinary situation. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. First appears in *Nick and the Glimmung*. Context: "Dick details the transition from the "Father" stage (1974 - Justice/Warrior) to the "Son" stage (1975 - Spring King/Dancing Youth)."

linked_segments.summary: Dick details the transition from the "Father" stage (1974 - Justice/Warrior) to the "Son" stage (1975 - Spring King/Dancing Youth). He posits that the "Light Creatures" have returned to the world afte
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_10.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick proposes a "Brain Colony" or "Grain Brain" model of the universe, where individual humans are interconnected cells in a vast compound eye of the Immanent Mind. He explores the "male womb" of Zeus
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_12.txt

reference.significance: The ordinary name in the extraordinary situation
